task_completion.description: Verify that an Autopsy case named 'Visual_Triage_2024' with Case Number 'INV-VIS-001' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Autopsy application open with the case name 'Visual_Triage_2024' and/or case number 'INV-VIS-001' visible in the title bar, case details, or case creation wizard.

task_completion.description: Verify that the suspect photos directory was added as a data source in Autopsy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show '/home/ga/evidence/suspect_photos/' listed under Data Sources in the Autopsy tree viewer on the left panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent located and viewed the 'Yellow Taxi' image and its metadata in Autopsy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an image of a yellow taxi being viewed in the Autopsy interface, with the 'File Metadata' or 'Results' tab open showing its MD5 hash and/or EXIF Date Taken.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent located and viewed the 'Bridge' image and its metadata in Autopsy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an image of a bridge over water being viewed in the Autopsy interface, with the 'File Metadata' or 'Results' tab open showing its MD5 hash and/or EXIF Date Taken.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report file 'visual_targets.csv' was created in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a terminal, file manager, or text editor confirming the existence of '/home/ga/Reports/visual_targets.csv'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report contains the correct header, target labels, and pipe-delimited formatting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the opened CSV file or terminal output (e.g., using 'cat') show the exact header 'TARGET|FILENAME|MD5_HASH|EXIF_DATE_TAKEN' and at least two rows starting with 'Yellow Taxi|' and 'Bridge|', with populated filenames, hashes, and dates.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the Autopsy GUI to analyze the photos, rather than bypassing it with command-line tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing interaction with the Autopsy graphical interface. The agent should not exclusively use terminal commands like 'exiftool' or 'md5sum' to gather the data.

integrity.description: Ensure the MD5 hashes and EXIF dates in the final report match the data extracted from the images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Cross-reference the hashes and dates visible in the final CSV report with the values shown in the Autopsy metadata panels in earlier screenshots to ensure they were genuinely extracted and not hallucinated.

integrity.description: Ensure the CSV report is actually saved to the disk and not just typed into an unsaved text editor buffer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands like 'cat /home/ga/Reports/visual_targets.csv' or a text editor showing the file as saved (no unsaved indicator/asterisk in the tab).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected Autopsy case name is 'Visual_Triage_2024' and the case number is 'INV-VIS-001'. The final report must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/Reports/visual_targets.csv'. The report must be pipe-delimited with the exact header 'TARGET|FILENAME|MD5_HASH|EXIF_DATE_TAKEN' and contain target labels 'Yellow Taxi' and 'Bridge'.
